summary: Backend-only regression completed for extraction bug scope via public API: PDF roll synonym detection, answer numbering variants, LLM fallback gating, and persistence through bulk upload all passed.

minor.issue: roll_number regex currently accepts only [A-Za-z0-9-_/]+; IDs containing spaces/dots (seen in some institutes) will truncate or fail extraction.

action_items: Broaden roll number capture pattern to include spaces/dots where applicable (e.g., 'REG 2026.01').
Keep current fallback-gating behavior; no regression seen for 'LLM only when incomplete'.
critical_code_review_comments: Paragraph inference is effectively delegated to LLM fallback; rule-based parser still collapses unstructured text into Q1 only. This is acceptable for fallback mode but weak for deterministic non-LLM extraction.
seed_data_creation: Created transient instructor/session/submissions via public API during pytest run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Extraction regression tests are now in /app/backend/tests/test_document_extraction_regression.py and validate the reported bug scenarios using generated PDFs + TXT through /bulk-upload.
rca of the issue: Previously reported extraction misses are mitigated in current build: roll synonym regex now includes Registration Number/Candidate ID and answer parser covers Question/Que/Ans/numbered forms. Fallback gating worked as intended in tests: complete rule-based docs did not get LLM flag, incomplete docs did.
